title: Default Environment Design

content.statement: Defaults drive behavior with minimal willpower.
content.question: What default in your environment is working against you?
content.life_lens: Healthy meals default; junk harder to reach.
sop: Walk through your physical and digital environment and note the path of least resistance for each habit you're trying to change.
Rate the Default Appeal and Friction for your desired behavior, 1-10 each (see calculation, page 5), how attractive is it by default, and how much effort does it take.
Remove friction from the behavior you want (pre-portion healthy food, auto-pay bills, pin the right app).
Add friction to the behavior you don't want (delete the app, move the snacks out of reach, require a manual step).
Re-check monthly, defaults erode as new conveniences and shortcuts sneak back in.
questions: What default in your environment is working against you?
What's the easiest option available to you right now in your space, and is it the one you actually want to default to?
If you removed all willpower from the equation, would your environment still lead you to the right choice?

definitions.def: The behavior that happens when no decision is made.

content.equation_example: Default Appeal (rate 1-10: how attractive the default option already is) divided by Friction (rate 1-10: how much effort it takes to do) equals Behavior likelihood. Default Appeal 8 divided by Friction 2 equals 4. Higher means the environment is doing the work for you without willpower; lower means you're relying on discipline to overcome a bad default.
content.closing_statement: Design the default so willpower isn't required.

caption:
Principle Nº 195: Default Environment Design

Defaults drive behavior with minimal willpower.

Ask yourself: What default in your environment is working against you?
